title: UIR — shared definitions
description: Types every layer depends on: identity, source locations, the node base contract, and the reserved diagnostic codes.

NodeId.description:
A node's content-addressed identity: blake3 of the node's canonical form, minus its own id, anchor and span.

Ids are permanent. They key overrides, caches, incrementality and AI provenance (ADR-7), so the canonical form that produces them may never change meaning.

Anchor.description:
A human-stable path to a node, e.g. `lib/screens/checkout.dart#CheckoutScreen/build/Column[0]/Row[2]`.

Stable across formatting-only edits. This is the key the override system uses, so a reformatted file must not orphan a human's hand-written component.

SourceSpan.description: A location in a Dart source file.

file.description: Project-relative path. Never absolute: an absolute path would make output depend on where the project sits on disk.

length.description: Length in characters. Absent means a point.

UirNodeBase.description:
The fields every UIR node carries (Spec §2.3).

Nodes reference this through `allOf`. Children are held in arrays, and **array order is semantic**: it is the order the widgets appear in, and every generator, serializer and equality check preserves it.

anchor.description: The override key, when the node is addressable by a human.

ext.description: Plugin extension data, namespaced `x-<plugin>`. Core passes round-trip it untouched (Spec §2.6).

TypeRef.description: A resolved Dart type, as the analyzer saw it.

library.description: The library that declares it, e.g. `package:flutter/material.dart`.

WidgetRef.description: A reference to a widget class — a framework widget, a package widget, or one the application declares.

library.description: The declaring library. Distinguishes a framework widget from a package's, and from the application's own.

userDefined.description:
Whether the application declares this widget.

C1 evidence: a user's own screens are what the compiler *generates*, not constructs it must map. Reporting them as unknown constructs was a false positive that would have opened every compatibility report with a lie.

DiagnosticCode.description:
Diagnostic codes reserved by the schema because a node or a pass carries them (Spec v2.1 §A6).

Only codes the UIR itself refers to are listed. The full registry lives in the compiler.

x-uir-enum-docs.BRG2301: A non-serializable object was passed as a route argument (ADR-11a). A URL route carries an identifier, not an object graph. Error.
x-uir-enum-docs.BRG2302: Cross-route state was promoted to a store by N11 (ADR-11). Informational — promotion is never silent.
x-uir-enum-docs.BRG2303: A cross-route callback could not be promoted; an override is required (ADR-11). Error.
